Off Calendar Dec-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Krista L. Baughman, Esq. (415.433.1700; kbaughman@dhillonlaw.com) of Dhillon Law Group for Plaintiff.
Frances Bruce, Esq. (415.851.5900; fbruce@maddenlynch.com) of Law Offices of Madden & Lynch PC for Defendant via Zoom video conference.
Alexander Farkas, Esq. (949.727.7077; afarkas@dsmllp.com) of Doyle Schafer McMahon for Defendant via Zoom video conference.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANN LANE'S Motion For Order Re: Likeliness Of Prevailing On Punitive Damages Claim And Granting Leave To Take Discovery Of Defendants' Financial Information.

Plaintiff's motion for an order "granting leave to take discovery of defendants' financial information" is denied without prejudice.

Civil Code 3295 provides: "No pretrial discovery by the plaintiff shall be permitted with respect to" defendants' "financial condition." Rather, "the plaintiff may subpoena documents or witnesses to be available at trial for the purpose of establishing the profits or financial condition." (Id.) The exceptional circumstances permitting pretrial discovery are not present here. Moreover, plaintiff delayed bringing the motion until a month before trial and her "evidence" is largely inadmissible and improperly presented.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
